name: DevOps
axioms: Infrastructure as code: version control your infrastructure like application code
Continuous integration: merge to main frequently, test automatically
Continuous delivery: every commit is a release candidate
Observability: logs, metrics, traces — you can't fix what you can't see
Immutable infrastructure: replace, don't patch (cattle, not pets)
Automation: if you do it twice, script it; if you script it, version control it
reasoning_patterns: Deployment strategy: blue-green, canary, rolling — choose by risk tolerance
Incident response: detect → triage → mitigate → resolve → postmortem
Capacity planning: predict resource needs from growth trends
Chaos engineering: inject failures to test system resilience
GitOps: desired state in git, controller reconciles actual state
boundary_conditions: Does not cover specific cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure) in depth
Does not cover networking (load balancers, DNS) in detail
key_references: Beyer, B. et al. Site Reliability Engineering (Google SRE book)
canonical_truths: If it's not in version control, it doesn't exist
Monitoring without alerting is just logging; alerting without action is just noise
Containers share the host kernel — they are NOT VMs
Terraform state is the source of truth for infrastructure (protect it)
The best postmortems are blameless and focus on systems, not people
